name: Nordea Bank AB - Bull Gull X5 Nordnet Fund
DNB OBX ETF
summary:
The fund has a passive investment strategy, which involves copying the composition and yield of the OBX index as closely as possible. The fund managers thus do not attempt to outperform the OBX index.
To ensure that the fund portfolio reflects the composition of the OBX index, the fund invests in the equities included in the OBX index with roughly the same weightings as the equities have in the OBX index. The fund invests in the 25 largest shares listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange.

name: XACT OBX
summary: XACT OBX is an exchange-traded fund (ETF) listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange. It aims to replicate the performance of the OBX index, which consists of the 25 most traded stocks on the Oslo Stock Exchange. The fund provides investors with a simple way to gain broad exposure to the Norwegian stock market.

name: XACT OBX Bear
summary: XACT OBX Bear is an exchange-traded fund (ETF) that aims to provide inverse exposure to the OBX index on the Oslo Stock Exchange. This means that the fund is designed to increase in value when the OBX index decreases, and vice versa. It is a leveraged product intended for short-term trading strategies.

name: XACT OBX Bull
summary: XACT OBX Bull is an exchange-traded fund (ETF) designed to provide leveraged exposure to the OBX index on the Oslo Stock Exchange. This means the fund aims to amplify the daily returns of the OBX index, typically by a factor of two. It is a leveraged product intended for short-term trading strategies and is not suitable for long-term investment.

name: XACT OBX ETF
summary: The fund is an exchange-traded index fund and its objective is to follow the performance of the OBX Index as closely as possible. OBX Index is a market-value weighted financial index consisting of the 25 most liquid equities on the Oslo stock exchange. The composition of the index is normally adjusted every six months with regard to the transferable securities included and the percentage of the index to ensure consistency with market performance. The selection criteria and other adjustments to the composition of the index are governed by the Oslo B√É¬∂rs Index Methodology.

name: XACT OBX Bear ETF
summary: The fund is an exchange-traded securities fund and its objective is to give a daily exposure that corresponds as closely as possible, but does not exceed, 200% of the daily opposite change in the OBX Index. This means that if the index declines by 1%, the value of the fund increases by approximately 2%, and if the index rises by 1%, the value of the fund decreases by approximately 2%. This objective does not constitute a guarantee by the fund or the Management Company that a certain return will be achieved. OBX Index is a market-value weighted financial index consisting of the 25 most liquid equities on the Oslo stock exchange. The composition of the index is normally adjusted every six months with regard to the transferable securities included and the percentage of the index to ensure consistency with market performance. The selection criteria and other adjustments to the composition of the index are governed by the Oslo B√É¬∂rs Index Methodology.
